landmines.title: Repo & R2 size
landmines.details: 5 MP3s tracked in git, digests/ is 67 MB.

landmines.title: RSS integrity, LFS absence, R2 host consistency
Legacy flat files in digests/
landmines.details: 95 legacy flat files pinned at digests/ top level. They cannot be moved (existing RSS URLs anchor to them), but no new files should land here.

landmines.title: TTS voice settings consistency
landmines.details: All shows match the _defaults.yaml voice baseline.

landmines.title: summaries_*.json live under digests/<slug>/
landmines.details: All summaries JSONs live under their per-show subdirectory.

trades.strategy: Sector rotation into mining on positive earnings reaction amid commodity supply disruptions
Analyst upgrade play on successful Phase 3 results and accelerated enrollment
Relative value rotation into US telecom after analyst reassessment highlights superior risk/reward versus European peer.
Balance-sheet-recapitalization play on $1.5 billion cash infusion combined with recent insider buying.
Earnings-surprise momentum play on AI memory chip demand
Valuation-driven core banking play ahead of Investor Day with attractive multiples relative to sector.
Contrarian fade of negative analyst sentiment following recent price weakness, betting on eventual relief-driven rebound.
Fundamental growth play on cloud-driven revenue and margin expansion amid memory-sector volatility.
Contrarian mean-reversion play on elevated short interest against a fundamentally improving digital banking franchise.

lessons_learned.observation: Even with a publicly confirmed binary catalyst, the position moved against us, likely because expectations were already elevated and broader sector momentum was lacking. This reinforces the critical process gap in data verification
lessons_learned.adjustment: Always verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed

lessons_learned.observation: Despite the positive clinical catalyst the position moved against us, underscoring that binary events can still produce losses when expectations are already elevated. This reinforces the need to verify pricing and liquidity data from multiple providers to accurately judge whether any simulated trade is generating alpha versus the NASDAQ Composite
lessons_learned.adjustment: Always verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed

lessons_learned.observation: Even when a binary catalyst such as accelerated enrollment is publicly confirmed, the market can still price in expectations ahead of the announcement and rotate out on the news, especially when broader sector momentum is absent. This reinforces the need to verify real-time order flow and liquidity before declaring any catalyst-driven trade closed
lessons_learned.adjustment: Verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed

lessons_learned.observation: The absence of reliable pricing data for LUCID blocked accurate tracking of whether the robotaxi thesis outperformed the NASDAQ, exposing a gap in execution
lessons_learned.adjustment: Verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed

lessons_learned.observation: The bid-ask-spread / order-flow-slippage lesson was taught in 6 of 8 recent episodes with nearly identical framing. Listeners tuned out after the third repeat.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Never re-teach bid_ask_spread or order_flow_slippage within their 45-day cooldown. If the only interesting mechanic today is a stale one, pick a different mechanic — freshness beats any single lesson.

lessons_learned.observation: The last 10 picks skewed 35% toward precious metals (Fortuna, SSR, West Red Lake) with zero flag raised on air. A real portfolio would have rebalanced.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Cap any single sector at 30% of the trailing 10-trade window. When the sector-concentration warning fires, the Practice Investment MUST pick a different sector that episode.

lessons_learned.observation: No episode has ever named the portfolio's alpha versus the NASDAQ Composite. Listeners have no way to judge whether the show is beating the market.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Every episode must state NASDAQ Composite level, portfolio YTD return, and YTD alpha in the Market Pulse segment. Fridays add the weekly alpha. Monthly episodes add month-to-date alpha.

lessons_learned.observation: Quick Hits consistently slipped into pure news reading (maple syrup shortage, Ackman's Universal Music bid, craft-brewer cost absorption) with no investing angle.
lessons_learned.adjustment: Every Quick Hit ends with an **Action:** line containing exactly one concrete portfolio decision stated as a verb phrase. Items without a concrete action are rejected at the digest stage.

lessons_learned.observation: Even a fundamental catalyst around robotaxi partnerships and bill-of-materials cost reductions cannot be properly scored when price data is missing, limiting our ability to judge whether the thesis was pricing in. This exposes a process vulnerability in data reliance that must be fixed to accurately track whether simulated picks are beating the NASDAQ
lessons_learned.adjustment: Verify data availability from multiple providers before declaring a weekly hold closed
